title: Localization Admission V1
objective: Decide whether Localization has enough positive-source evidence and cross-project recurrence to admit an engine-light, renderer-neutral reusable family before any package identity exists.
scope: Authoritative standards research, reusable boundary derivation, standard entry, and an admit, revise, or reject decision
non_goals: No product copy, translation delivery, package directory, package ID, implementation, compatibility, or device claim

checkpoints.title: Research authoritative Localization standards and shipped practices
checkpoints.outcome: A public positive-source comparison covers locale identity, fallback, formatting, plural and select behavior, bidirectional text, fonts, accessibility, content pipeline, and engine boundaries.
non_goals: No architecture decision, package identity, implementation, or popularity-only source selection
do_not_touch: Product-localized content and private consumer material

required_capabilities: Can compare standards, official engine documentation, shipped practices, licenses, conformance boundaries, and conflicting guidance
required_tools: Web access, Git, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: A public source matrix with provenance, authority, strengths, exclusions, and unresolved conflicts

acceptance.criterion: Sources cover language tags, locale data and fallback, plural or select formatting, bidirectional text, fonts, accessibility, content exchange, and Unity integration boundaries.
Every source records provenance, authority, license or usage boundary, strengths, exclusions, and conflicts.
Reusable behavior is separated from project-owned copy, narrative, translations, and art assets.

verification.procedure: Review the source matrix against the declared coverage and inspect every link at a recorded date.
verification.expected: The matrix uses positive authoritative sources, names gaps, and makes no implementation or admission claim.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Ask a maintainer to admit LOC-01 as Ready on issue #75 after confirming the source coverage and bounded write set.

checkpoints.title: Derive the renderer-neutral architecture and standard entry
checkpoints.outcome: Positive sources yield a reviewable message identity, locale resolution, fallback, formatting, validation, failure, content-pipeline, Unity-adapter, and renderer boundary without package creation.
non_goals: No package directory, package ID, implementation, or compatibility promise
do_not_touch: Consumer content, translations, and product UI

required_capabilities: Can derive engine-light domain, content, adapter, renderer, validation, and version-regeneration boundaries from positive evidence
qualification_evidence: A source-to-requirement map and independent architecture review

acceptance.criterion: The standard entry states position, applies_when, does_not_apply_when, deliverables, acceptance, common_misuses, and bounded related_entries.
Message identity, locale resolution, fallback, formatting inputs, validation, and failure behavior remain engine-light.
Content pipeline, Unity integration, and renderer responsibilities are explicit optional boundaries.

verification.procedure: Trace every mandatory boundary to admitted LOC-01 evidence and conduct an independent architecture review.
verification.expected: The architecture is reusable, source-backed, renderer-neutral, and contains no package or compatibility claim.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for LOC-01 completion, then request maintainer Ready admission for LOC-02 in issue #76.

checkpoints.title: Decide admission and project implementation checkpoints
checkpoints.outcome: A maintainer records an evidence-backed admit, revise, or reject decision and projects later implementation checkpoints only when admission succeeds.
non_goals: No automatic admission, empty package scaffold, compatibility claim, or device claim
do_not_touch: Automatic Ready, merge, release, and permission settings

required_capabilities: Can review system admission, provenance, reusable boundaries, implementation gates, and public authority
required_tools: Git, GitHub Issue and Project read access, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: A criterion-by-criterion admission receipt and independent maintainer decision

acceptance.criterion: Every admission criterion references public LOC-01 and LOC-02 evidence.
Admit, revise, or reject preserves common kernel, configurable variation, exclusions, provenance, and exact next action.
Only an admitted result may project implementation checkpoints or later package identities.

verification.procedure: Run the full repository validator and independently review the admission record, queue transition, and projected next work.
verification.expected: The decision is evidence-backed, authority-safe, and creates no empty implementation surface.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for LOC-01 and LOC-02 completion, then request the LOC-03 integration lease in issue #77.

integration.strategy: The maintainer reviews the complete source and architecture trail, records admit, revise, or reject, and authorizes package identities only after admission.
